_schema.description: Frozen German exonym lookup for institution/government/agency proper nouns, seeding the translation stage's Turn-1 glossary to enforce ONE consistent German rendering per entity (fixes the bake-off terminology/exonym drift, e.g. 'Hormus' vs 'Hormuz'). Person names are NOT here (they pass through). keep_original=true marks proper-noun orgs/brands that stay identical in German (e.g. Hamas, OpenAI, Amnesty International).
_schema.key_format: Case-insensitively normalised English name: lowercased, leading 'the' dropped, periods removed, parenthetical acronyms and possessive 's stripped, whitespace collapsed. Trivial variants ('US State Department','US state department','U.S. State Department') collapse to one key ('us state department').
_schema.structure: key -> {canonical_en, de, source, keep_original}. source in {official (org's own German name), wikipedia (German Wikipedia article title), opus (no established German exonym; anthropic/claude-opus-4.6 translation — review these)}.
_schema.provenance: Harvested from actors[].name/final_actors[].name across output/*/tp-*.json (63 TPs). Resolved via cascade A(official)>B(wikipedia)>C(opus). FROZEN: the translation loop consumes this, never edits it; unseen entities fall back to the run-time glossary and are logged for manual addition.
